summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: CCCA seeks new path to passage
articles.description: Sens. Dick Durbin and Roger Marshall are angling to attach their credit card interchange fee legislation to a major housing bill before Congress.

articles.title: Bill open to M&A deals, CEO says
articles.description: Consolidation in business payments and finance automation affirms a growing interest in the space, the software firm’s founder said.

articles.title: Amex taps customers for fraud fight
articles.description: Conversations with cardholders are crucial in developing countermeasures to deter scammers, a top executive for the card company said.

articles.title: Would a bank buy PayPal?
articles.description: Whether major banks, such as JPMorgan Chase and Capital One, might have an interest in owning PayPal could turn on their ability to monetize that two-sided network.

articles.title: Shift4 taps xAI for customer service
articles.description: The payment processor announced it would partner with Elon Musk's artificial intelligence company for a range of uses.

articles.title: Apple, Google wallets get personal
articles.description: Beyond payments, the digital wallet is a platform for commerce with software startup Badge trying to help brands expand their wallet presence.

articles.title: Amex doubles down on NYC
articles.description: The credit card juggernaut plans to build a new global headquarters in Manhattan, becoming the owner and only occupant of a 55-floor skyscraper.

articles.title: Inside Toast’s 2025 hiring binge
articles.description: The point-of-sale provider added 800 employees in 2025 as others were making steep cuts.
